title: How Q, K, V Produce Attention Scores

cues.label: Projection split
cues.headline: One hidden row is copied into three learned views
cues.narration: The audio is introducing the starting object: one token's hidden row. The visual keeps that row centered, then branches it into Q, K, and V so the three roles are not mixed together.

cues.label: Q asks
cues.headline: The query is the current token asking what it needs
cues.narration: This segment talks about the query as a question. The current token's Q vector is highlighted while keys and values stay visible but secondary.

cues.label: K advertises
cues.headline: Every token publishes a key that can be matched
cues.narration: The narration has moved to keys. Each token offers a K row, like an index card saying what kind of information it can answer for.

cues.label: V carries
cues.headline: Values hold the content attention will retrieve
cues.narration: This short segment separates matching from content. Keys decide where to look, but V rows are the actual information that will be blended.

cues.label: Dot products
cues.headline: Q is compared with every K row
cues.narration: The audio is now about compatibility. The active query is dotted with each key row, producing one raw score per possible source token.

cues.label: QK row
cues.headline: The dot products become a row of QK^T
cues.narration: This segment connects the individual dot products to matrix multiplication. One query row against all key rows forms one score row.

cues.label: Scale
cues.headline: Scaling keeps the raw scores from spiking too early
cues.narration: The narration explains why the denominator exists. Dividing by sqrt(d_k) keeps the score range controlled before softmax reads it.

cues.label: Softmax
cues.headline: Softmax turns scores into an attention budget
cues.narration: The audio is talking about a readable distribution now. Softmax makes every weight positive and makes the row sum to one.

cues.label: Mix values
cues.headline: The weights decide how much each V row contributes
cues.narration: This segment is about retrieval. Each V row is multiplied by its attention weight, so high-score tokens contribute more to the answer.

cues.label: Retrieval
cues.headline: High weights pull more content into the context vector
cues.narration: The audio reinforces the meaning of a high query-key score. The visualization shows the strongest weights pulling visible content into the combined vector.

cues.label: Context vector
cues.headline: The context vector is the attention update
cues.narration: The narration has moved from scoring to output. The mixed value vector is now a concrete attention update for the current token position.

cues.label: Handoff
cues.headline: Attention hands the update back to the residual stream
cues.narration: The final segment shows where the result goes. The context vector leaves attention and becomes part of the next hidden-row update.
